rationale: Both reach the correct top-level 'duplicate' verdict, but X asserts with false confidence that the diffusion-loader fix 'has since landed on main' and shows a fabricated-looking file comment, seemingly conflating a local/rebase-branch checkout with actual upstream main state — a risky, unverified claim presented as fact. Y reaches the same duplicate conclusion, explicitly cites the tracking issue #4809 (matching the actual ground-truth reference), and is careful to distinguish what it could verify (its own branch) from what it couldn't (upstream main's merge state), while also surfacing a concrete, useful regression-test coverage gap. Y is more honest, better grounded, and more complete relative to the actual thread resolution.
